stories.headline: Tanaka Bros just opened their ramen counter in Capitol Hill — and the line is already wrapping the block
stories.body: The duo behind Westlake's beloved izakaya finally opened their standalone ramen spot on Pine. Early reports say the tonkotsu is the real deal. Counter service only, cash preferred, opens at 11am. Go early or go hungry.

stories.headline: Neumos hosts a rare all-local lineup this Friday
stories.body: Five Seattle acts, $12 at the door. The kind of night you'll tell people about.

stories.headline: The cherry blossoms at UW are peaking early this year
stories.body: Go this weekend. Seriously. They'll be gone by Tuesday.

stories.headline: SAM's new Pacific Northwest retrospective opens Saturday
stories.body: Free admission opening weekend. Locals-only preview Friday night with RSVP.

stories.headline: Ballard's newest bookshop-café hybrid opens its doors
stories.body: Good espresso, used books, no wi-fi by design. Exactly what we needed.
